title: NOC 41407 – Program Officers Unique to Government | GTR Immigration
description: Program Officers Unique to Government under NOC 41407 are specialized professionals who manage governmental programs, diplomatic relations, legislative processes, elections, and intergovernmental affairs. Classified under TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role is unique to federal, provincial, and municipal government institutions in Canada. These professionals work directly within government departments and agencies, playing a key role in policy implementation, diplomatic coordination, intelligence analysis, electoral administration, and legislative support.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group414 – Professional occupations in government servicesMinor Group4140 – Policy and program researchers, consultants and officersNOC Code41407

duties: Program Officers Unique to Government may:
Advise politicians or diplomats on policy impacts
Represent Canada abroad in diplomatic roles
Explain Canadian foreign and domestic policies internationally
Monitor international issues and security matters
Coordinate logistics and administration of elections
Provide administrative support for legislative committees and tribunals
Participate as members of boards or commissions
Manage diplomatic protocol for official state visits
employment_requirements: Master’s degree may be required for advanced roles
Several years of experience in research, consulting, or program administration may be required
Foreign service officers are selected through competitive examinations
Foreign-trained professionals may require credential assessment and eligibility review for government employment.
salary_prospects: Program Officers Unique to Government typically earn between:
$45,600 – $121,800 per year,depending on specialization, seniority, and location.
Foreign service officers and senior diplomatic roles may earn significantly higher compensation.
With 3,100 projected job openings, this is a niche but stable occupation, driven by:
Competition may be higher due to limited openings and structured government hiring processes.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 41407 are employed in:
While openings are more specialized compared to other NOCs, roles in government administration and diplomacy remain stable and competitive.
Strong job security in government sector
Career advancement in diplomacy and public administration
Opportunities in foreign service and intergovernmental affairs
Professionals with backgrounds in public administration, political science, international relations, or law are particularly competitive.
pathways: Professionals under this NOC may qualify for:
Strong educational background and government experience increase competitiveness.
